post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 212: Death's Edge

Story
Welcome all to Death's Edge!

You are all here because you lost your soul in some way. Maybe you just died, or you sold it to the devil, or there was a tragic accident. In any case, you're here. However, not all hope is lost. Your soul still exists. It never really goes away. It never really dies. Even if you don't realize it, it will still keep on living; living without a body. And speaking of bodies, look at yourself! You've all been given wonderful new bodies, complete with whatever your soul desired. How lovely. It's a welcome gift from me. Enjoy it while you can.

On the ferry to your final resting place, each and every single one of you gets a second chance; a second life. Everyone here will be able to gamble for their lives. Isn't it exciting? Everyone will get 100 chips to play with; who cares if you were a beggar or a millionaire in your life, everyone is equal in death! Besides, your memory has been mostly wiped; that's why you don't even remember who you are! However, your skills, wits, and brainpower have been maintained from your life. You can try and use that to figure out who you are, but it's probably a better idea to use them to outwit your opponents.

That's right; not all of you can go back to the surface. It costs 500 chips to be reincarnated. Just outwit a value of four people and you can go back; pretty simple, right? However, this gambling isn't risk free. If you can't or just choose not to go back to the overworld, then your chip count at the end of the journey will determine where you'll go. It determines where your soul will rescind for… well, the rest of time, and beyond. In other worlds, forever. If you have zero chips, down to the depths of hell you go. If you gather up all 1500 chips… well, you might not deserve to go to the upper echelons of heaven, but you'll still go there anyways. And anything in between gets you a wide variety of afterlives. However, I'll let you in on a little secret; 100 chips is good enough for an "okay" afterlife. So, if you want to be a wimp, you can just try and keep your initial pot.

You can gamble in whatever game you want, as long as everyone agrees to the rules. You can even transfer chips at any time if you'd like, though I have no idea why you'd not want to be selfish. In addition, there's one more rule: you must participate in at least one gamble every day. If you don't? Well your chip count gets zeroed and your journey to the afterlife ends. In addition, if you are caught cheating, your chip count will also get zeroed, and will be distributed evenly to the other players in the gamble you were playing in. Well, what are y'all waiting for? Grab your chips and get gambling! Don't cheat, and good luck!


Read all of the rules.

Voting and Hangs
  • You must vote using the format of 
    Vote: NAME
  • You may unvote by doing 
    Unvote
  • You may vote for no hang to occur (thereby skipping the day) by doing 
    Vote: Sleep
  • Votes will only count if they are visible; that is, not embedded within a hide tag or the like.
  • Any invalid vote will be completely ignored.
  • For the purposes of voting, the letters I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) will be considered the same; this goes for any other similar looking symbols. Don't test me on what is allowed; if it looks like a valid vote, it is a valid vote.
  • If a player receives the majority of the votes, that player will be hanged and the day will end immediately.
  • If at least half of the players vote to skip the day, then no hang will occur and the day will end immediately.
  • If no one person receives the majority of the votes by the end of the day, the person with the most votes at that time will be hanged. If there are multiple such people, then a person is randomly hanged from among those with the most votes.
  • There is no majority during Day 1. That means a hang will only happen at the end of the day.
  • As soon as the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted.
  • Votes are locked when there is exactly one more town member alive than mafia.
  • Any incorrect votecounts made will be counted as the correct votecount if the mistake is not noticed and corrected before the next votecount.


Activity and Deadlines
  • Each Day will be 3 days long.
  • Each Night will be 1 day long.
  • The start and end of each Day will be at 1:00 AM UTC (9:00 PM ET).
  • I will give out PM Prods at my discretion; there is no official time limit, but I may start looking for a replacement as soon as 24 hours after I give a PM Prod.
  • If you post before I find a replacement (for your inactivity), you will not be replaced.


List of Don’t’s
  • Don’t post if you are not participating in this game.
  • Don’t post at night.
  • Don’t talk about this game to anyone except me (the mod) outside of this thread, unless you are given explicit permission by me (the mod) to do so.
  • Don’t edit or delete posts.
  • Don’t conceal text with things such as transparent text and the like; hide tags are alright to use.
  • Don’t post if you are dead.
  • Don’t be immature.
  • Don't be inactive or flake.
  • Don’t gamethrow.
  • Don’t imitate the mod.
  • Don't ask me (the mod) questions in thread about game mechanics; please ask them in your Role PM, and if I deem it necessary I will publicly clarify the mechanics of the game.
  • Don't speculate about the reasons behind a warning, replacement or modkill.
  • Don’t argue with the mod about warnings, replacements, or modkills.
  • Don't angle shoot.
  • Don’t abuse loopholes.

Violation of any of these rules will result in a warning, a force-replacement, and/or a modkill. I reserve the right to give warnings to, force-replace, and/or modkill anyone for whatever reason. Please do not make me do this.

Warnings
mark888 receives a warning for posting at night.
awesomemimg327. receives a warning for posting at night.
MY-2 receives a warning for posting at night.
etr217 receives a warning for posting at night.
ARay10 receives a warning for inactivity.
KaiDuck receives a warning for inactivity.
snoozingnewt receives a warning for angle-shooting.
tikkitamatoa receives a warning for angle-shooting.
MY-2 receives a warning for angle-shooting.
Osmium_ receives a warning for angle-shooting.
etr217 receives a warning for inactivity.
mathjay receives a warning for inactivity.
Osmium_ receives a warning for inactivity.
sudzoof receives a warning for inactivity.
mathjay receives a warning for inactivity.


Setup
This game will use the JOAT^2 setup. Some of you might recognize this as the Mafia Universe Championship Season 7 Setup.
  • There are 15 players.
  • There are 10 Vanilla Townies, 2 Jack of All Trades, and 3 Mafia Goons.
  • The Jack of All Trades have three abilities: Doctor, Tracker, Motion Detector. All three abilities must be used once before any can be repeated again. In other words, at any given time, any specific ability may not be used more than once more than another ability.
  • There is a deadline hang.
  • When there is exactly one more townie than mafia alive, votes become locked.
  • Mafia factional kills are required and assigned. Mafia are allowed to proxy kills; that is, one member can submit for another member to carry the kill.
  • Mafia have daychat.
  • Mafia win when they have parity; that is, when their numbers equals to or exceeds that of town's.

Role PMs
Vanilla Townie wrote:
You are a Vanilla Townie.

You have no special powers. Your power is your voice and your vote. Good luck!

You win when all mafia members are dead and there is at least one town member alive.
Jack of All Trades wrote:
You are a Jack of All Trades.

Every night, you may use one of the following abilities. You cannot repeat an ability until you use each ability once.

Doctor: Target a player; that player will be protected from the night kill. You may not target yourself, and cannot target the same player two nights in a row.
Tracker: Target a player; learn who they visited that night, if any. The result will be in the form of "PLAYER visited PLAYER" or "PLAYER did not visit anyone."
Motion Detector: Target a player; learn whether or not they either visited someone, or were visited by someone other than you. The result will be in the form of "There was motion around PLAYER" or "There was no motion around PLAYER."

You win when all mafia members are dead and there is at least one town member alive.
Mafia Goon wrote:
You are a Mafia Goon. Your teammates are NAME, a Mafia Goon, and NAME, a Mafia Goon.

You may talk with your teammates in this QuickTopic, though other forms of communication (such as Discord) are also fine, as long as I (the mod) is in it, as well as all members of mafia.

Every night, a mafia member may submit a factional night kill on a target. If successful, the target player will die.

You win when mafia's numbers equals to or exceeds that of town's.

  • summitwei - Jack of All Trades - Night 5
  • sheepsaysmeep - Mafia Goon - Day 6

mark888 has jumped off the ferry.

Night 3 begins.

Day 4 begins.

"Hey, so, what do you say? Isn't that a great plan?"

"I mean, I guess? What makes you think no one else has come up with this?"

"You moron look around! Does it look like ANYONE has a plan? They're all scared little fishes. All of them are distrustful of each other. We can just win and split the winnings and all go back."

"Ye, I got some unfinished business I need to take care of. Plus, I think you two are up for the job, so it shouldn't be any problem."

"Let's split up soon; we wouldn't want the others to suspect that we're working together. We can meet up some other time. See ya!"

"Goodbye."

post_rendered:
The first day was… jittery at best. Everyone just got to know each other. A few low stakes games were played; nothing super major. However, near the end of the day, on the twentieth hour, an intense, high-stakes game of a very classic game: goofspiel.

The rules of goofspiel are very simple: Each player starts with a hand consisting of cards from 1-13. Then, a deck consisting of prize cards, numbered from 1-13, will be shuffled. In every round, a prize card will be drawn from the deck and reveal. Each player will then privately bid on the prize card using one of the cards in their hand. Whoever bids higher will win the prize card. Whoever has a higher sum of prize cards wins the game.

Since this game wasn't a betting game, there was only one logic conclusion: both players would bet everything they had. Every single one of their chips. They each figured, if they were to bet something, why not bet it all? They were lucky to find someone that thought the same way as them. Someone who thought they would 100% win. Someone was wrong.

mark888 and sheepsaysmeep played in this battle of the wits. An incorporeal host was called, and they appeared from the void. A deck was produced, and each player was given their hands. The prize cards got shuffled, and with a soft thud it was placed on the table. Each player tossed in every single one of their chips on the table: a total of two hundred. The first prize card, a nice, solid 8, was revealed. The game was afoot.

The wasn't some sort of fighting game; as the game went on, the crowd got quieter and quieter. As each prize card was revealed, and each player placed down their bids, everyone waited with bated breath. After a while, it was down to the final two cards. The score was 41 to 35, in favor of mark888. The final two cards were and 6 and a 9, in some order. mark888 had only a 4 and a 8 remaining. sheepsaysmeep had a 5 and a 10. mark888 only had to secure one more prize card to win, but he had worse remaining cards. It looked to be just down to a flip of a coin. However, neither player believed this. Both of them tried as hard as they could, thinking of how their opponent was thinking. Thinking about thinking. The penultimate prize card turned out to be a 9. However, this was the final meaningful round. The last round where decisions mattered. The ultimate round was just a formality; a victory lap for whoever outwitted their opponent in this round.

sheepsaysmeep placed down their card first, with as best of a poker face he could manage, putting their hand over their mouth. mark888 soon followed with a slight smirk on his face. The dealer then nonchalantly took both cards, and revealed them. Even though it only took a second, the flip felt like an entirety. After all, this gamble would decide the fates of one of them for eternity. mark888's bid was a 4. sheepsaysmeep's bid was a 5.

A moment of silence passed. Then another. The dealer broke the silence by revealing the final prize card; everyone knew what it was anyways: a 6. mark888 said the first words: "I resign." He promptly passed all the chips into sheepsaysmeep's side of the table and got up. "Hey where are you going?" awesomeming327. said to mark888. mark888 didn't answer. He looked off the side of the ferry. Instead of a beautiful blue sea as one might expect, it was a dark, grey void. Void as far as the eye could see. Eerily silent.

"Hey what are you-" exclaimed bigmath, but before anyone could stop mark888, he climbed the railing and jumped off the ship. A few people peeked over. There was no sign of him. Nothing left of him besides a memory. A memory of a lost soul, waiting for their time to arrive. Waiting, waiting so patiently until they could no longer handle it, and accelerated their fall into the depths.

A few more moments passed. No announcement of any sort was made. Death was not an abnormal part of whatever game these souls were made to play. Maybe even expected. If such a voluntary death would not be acknowledged, what's stopping someone from getting violent? Killing someone else for the purposes of a better afterlife. One meant for moral beings, enjoyed by those without morals. These restless souls would have many thoughts on their minds as they drifted into sleep, waiting for the next day to come; the next day of this gloomy, seemingly never-ending ferry ride.

mark888 has jumped off the ferry. He was a Vanilla Townie.

post_rendered: Night 1 has begun. Please submit your actions, if you have any. Please note that I will take the most recent action that you have submitted.
post_canonical: [color=blue]Night 1 has begun. Please submit your actions, if you have any. Please note that I will take the most recent action that you have submitted.

post_rendered:
At seven hours past midnight, a loud gong rang throughout the ferry. People woke up, some eager to play some more, while others just hoped to die again.

One person would see that hope come true.

More than twelve hours passed until someone noticed something. Everyone was just gambling, or using the ferry's many facilities. No one knew each other that much, even after an entire day had passed. Everyone seemed to forget that they were dead. They more or less accepted the circumstances they were put in, even if it did take them a while. Who could blame them? No religion told them this was how death worked.

mathjay approached atomicpianowitch's room.

"Hey, are you in there? I haven't seen you since yesterday. You know you have to take part in at least one game a day, right?"

No answer. He banged on the door even harder.

"Hello???"

Still nothing.

██████ stopped mathjay from banging on the door even harder.

"Look, just leave him be; if he doesn't want to come out, he won't. There's nothing you can do to force them out."

"I suppose so…"

"No, how could you just think things are just fine in there? I'm going to break down this door if it's the last thing I'll do."

kevinmathz took a few steps back before ██████ stopped them.

"Fine, if you want to get in you can at least do it in a less destructive way."

She pulled out two paper clips and started picking the lock. After a few minutes, it opened.

"Hey atomicpianowitch? Why weren't you answe-"

"What? Why'd you stop tal-"

In front of them was atomicpianowitch, sleeping soundly in bed. A white blanket, neatly covered over him, with a beautiful red pattern near the center, clearly highlighting the centerpiece of the entire attraction: a knife, stabbed neatly into atomicpianowitch's torso.

"Ah… I see. Everyone, just stay calm. Stay away from the body. We need to have a meeting. Sure, we might be dead, but gosh darn it if I don't still have some moral sense. mathjay and kevinmathz, go get the rest of the people; I want to have a talk."

██████ walked a bit closer to the body, and pulled out the knife. She carefully looked at it. It appeared to be a standard kitchen knife. ██████ threw open the blanket and inspected the body a bit closer. Nothing out of the ordinary; no signs of a struggle. At least atomicpianowitch died peacefully in their sleep. Maybe that's how he went out in the overworld too. ██████ set the knife aside, and sat in a chair, seemingly in deep thought.

After a while, mathjay and kevinmathz came back, along with everyone else. A few gasps could be heard, before ██████ cleared her throat and stood up.

"So, the situation should be obvious. Someone has been murdered. I'm pretty confident our hosts didn't do this, so there's only one option. One of you did it. Maybe more. But the point is, at least one of us did."

"Hey!" someone from the audience shouted "Why couldn't it be you? Didn't you find the body first?"

"Well, for one, kevinmathz and I were with her when we found the body. Even though she might have done it, finding the body first isn't incriminating enough evidence."

"That's right." ██████ responded. "Plus, there's the mystery of how someone got into the room in the first place. I know atomicpianowitch was alive yesterday, because I saw him shortly before the final hour of the day. When mathjay, kevinmathz, and I arrived, the door was locked."

kevinmathz spoke up. "Oh, I think I have an explanation for that." He pulled out a small key. "I found this in the captain's headquarters while looking around for people. I think it's a master key. It wasn't locked up behind any door, so anyone could've easily grabbed it and returned it after the deed was done."

kevinmathz walked towards the lock. Sure enough, the key was able to lock and unlock it.

"Well," continued ██████, "I guess that explains how the murderer got in. I think we should keep this key in a safe place. In any case, I would like to first ask everyone the same few questions: Where were you last night, what were you doing, and when did you last see atomicpianowitch?"

One person spoke, and then a few more followed. What was supposed to be a civilized discussion grew more and more intense as time passed. The purpose of the ferry was supposedly to be fun. An enjoyable journey into the afterlife. However, with not just lives, but souls on the line, it was no time to be having fun. If these souls wished for a happy afterlife, they would have to catch whoever killed atomicpianowitch, and deliver swift justice. Even though no one mentioned this, it was clear that everyone knew what the punishment for murder should be: death. And so, in death, these tiny souls, in a random part of the unknown, fought for their lives, harder than they ever had while alive in the above. All this, just to engage in their own form of justice, when reality had no such rules in place.

atomicpianowitch has been stabbed. He was a Vanilla Townie.

post_rendered:
ਾਰੇ ਕਾਰੋਬਾਰਾਂ ਦਾ ਜੈਕ!

As usual I promised to do something during the night, and as usual, I did not end up doing as much as I wanted. But it's okay. I made a chart in the meantime:

I: awesomeming327.
II: bigmath
II: etr217
III: Charge
III: mathjay
III: MY-2
III: snoozingnewt
III: sudzoof
IV: sheepsaysmeep
IV: tikkitamatoa
V: kevinmathz
V: summitwei
V: atomicpianowitch
V: mark888
V: Osmium_

Each tier represents a class of players. Importantly, V class is all confirmed conftown. So I don't want to see any kevinmathz wagons, and if you don't trust me in the first place, then have fun trying to win. But while providing a neat organizational structure, my major conclusion with this listing is that I am 99% sure there must exist at least one scum in class III, and it may be a good idea to focus on this set of "relatively less explored players". Some explanation for this may surface in the notes (personal opinions) below.

Notes:
1. Look, there really isn't that much redeeming for awesomeming327.. I simply cannot make a good case for them being town. Post #963 is the best one can get. post_rendered:
wrt bigmath

the main scumtell ive noticed is, like, the opposite of "show don't tell". theres a lot of "i should do x", "i will do y", and no follow up. busywork ends up feeling performative
eg sequences like "i should read", "im going to go read now", "here i am reading", "it's so difficult to read", "hey look at me i finished reading". im exaggerating, but hopefully the point is clear.

secondly he's far too responsive to pressure. a) focusing all his efforts onto talking about the push on him back when it was very small and undeveloped b) shifting his reads to appease people asking about them- puts me in null, asked about it, moves me to town five posts later. this happened extensively so that his second list is clearly moreso trying to have a list that the public likes this time over real shifts that suddenly happened. i also didnt like the saying "these three names are null and also everyoen else is null" in the original list fwiw. c) i dont buy that he thinks his push is wolf driven; it comes off just as discrediting the push on him for a random bs reason. like, if he said "x and y hopped onto me. i think they are wolves" then that would be fine in italics. just going "scum are part of the 50 people wolfreading me glgl" and not diving any further isnt a genuine "hey guys i think im onto something", it's just "you shouldnt vote me because i think wolves are pushing me." he doesnt do anything with that take besides attempt to use it to self defend.

post_rendered:
Everyone was on the top deck. However, they weren't all gathered for a game. Under the deep grey sky, everyone only had one thought on their mind: atomicpianowitch. And of course, more importantly, who the killer was.

"Hey awesomeming327., why did you do it?" Osmium_ barked.

"I promise you I'm innocent! I don't know where these accusations are coming from!"

etr217 stepped forward a bit. "Well, for one, you have no abili."

"But I do! I literally said I was sleeping in my room! How could I have possibly killed atomicpianowitch when I was in my room! It was locked too so there's no way I could have gotten out and killed him."

"..." etr217 facepalmed. "You realize you can unlock your room from the inside, right? And in any case, there's no one that can support your alibi."
"But that's not incriminating evidence right? I know of plenty of other people who did the exact same thing as me!"

"Yes, that by itself is not enough to prove you are guilty. However, there is something peculiar that you did. tikkitamatoa saw you go into the kitchen late at night yesterday."

"Did he?"

tikkitamatoa responded "Yes, I did."

"So…" etr217 continued "Why did you go there?"

"How is this even relevant? I just went to get a drink of water."

"Don't play dumb. The murder weapon was a kitchen knife. And during our investigations, there was a missing knife in the kitchen. Therefore, the only people who could have gotten their hands on the knife used to kill atomicpianowitch are the people who entered the kitchen."

"What kind of argument is that? Couldn't there have been other people who entered the kitchen?"

"Well," etr217 asked the crowd "Did anyone else see anyone other than awesomeming327. go into the kitchen?"

The question was met with silence.

"Well, that proves it. There is literally no other person, other than awesomeming327., who could have murdered atomicpianowitch. Are there any objections?"

"YES!" awesomeming327. shouted.

"Not from you; I meant from the others."

Another question met with silence.

"Well, then let's get to the execution."

"WAIT WAIT I PROMISE I'M ACTUALLY INNOCENT I'VE BEEN FRAMED YOU GUYS LISTEN TO ME I CAN EXPL- MMMMM MMMMMM!"

tikkitamatoa duct taped him before any more could be said.

"MMMM MMM MMMMM MMM MMMMM MM!"

"Okay, let's just dump him overboard. I don't want to deal with awesomeming327.'s muffled screaming any longer than I have to."

Everyone agreed with etr217. Osmium_ and summitwei picked up awesomeming327. and started carrying him to the edge of the ship. On the way there, everyone was mostly silent. Of course, besides awesomeming327..

"M MMMM MMM MMMM! MMMMMMMM!"

It wasn't long before they arrived at the edge of the ship, though it certainly felt like awesomeming327.'s screaming was going to continue forever.

tikkitamatoa ripped the duct tape off of awesomeming327..

"Any last words?"

"I'M INNOCENT I'M TELLING YOU LET ME G-"

tikkitamatoa put the duct tape back on.

"Okay I regret asking him that question. Let's just toss this trash out."

Osmium_ and summitwei nodded in agreement and threw awesomeming327.'s squirming body overboard. Presumably, to the same place where mark888 went, if any. To a place where the dead are forgotten, even by the dead.

awesomeming327. has been thrown overboard. He was a Vanilla Townie.
post_canonical:
Everyone was on the top deck. However, they weren't all gathered for a game. Under the deep grey sky, everyone only had one thought on their mind: atomicpianowitch. And of course, more importantly, who the killer was.

"Hey awesomeming327., why did you do it?" Osmium_ barked.

"I promise you I'm innocent! I don't know where these accusations are coming from!"

etr217 stepped forward a bit. "Well, for one, you have no abili."

"But I do! I literally said I was sleeping in my room! How could I have possibly killed atomicpianowitch when I was in my room! It was locked too so there's no way I could have gotten out and killed him."

"..." etr217 facepalmed. "You realize you can unlock your room from the inside, right? And in any case, there's no one that can support your alibi."
"But that's not incriminating evidence right? I know of plenty of other people who did the exact same thing as me!"

"Yes, that by itself is not enough to prove you are guilty. However, there is something peculiar that you did. tikkitamatoa saw you go into the kitchen late at night yesterday."

"Did he?"

tikkitamatoa responded "Yes, I did."

"So…" etr217 continued "Why did you go there?"

"How is this even relevant? I just went to get a drink of water."

"Don't play dumb. The murder weapon was a kitchen knife. And during our investigations, there was a missing knife in the kitchen. Therefore, the only people who could  have gotten their hands on the knife used to kill atomicpianowitch are the people who entered the kitchen."

"What kind of argument is that? Couldn't there have been other people who entered the kitchen?"

"Well," etr217 asked the crowd "Did anyone else see anyone other than awesomeming327. go into the kitchen?"

The question was met with silence.

"Well, that proves it. There is literally no other person, other than awesomeming327., who could have murdered atomicpianowitch. Are there any objections?"

"YES!" awesomeming327. shouted.

"Not from you; I meant from the others."

Another question met with silence.

"Well, then let's get to the execution."

"WAIT WAIT I PROMISE I'M ACTUALLY INNOCENT I'VE BEEN FRAMED YOU GUYS LISTEN TO ME I CAN EXPL- MMMMM MMMMMM!"

tikkitamatoa duct taped him before any more could be said.

"MMMM MMM MMMMM MMM MMMMM MM!"

"Okay, let's just dump him overboard. I don't want to deal with awesomeming327.'s muffled screaming any longer than I have to."

Everyone agreed with etr217. Osmium_ and summitwei picked up awesomeming327. and started carrying him to the edge of the ship. On the way there, everyone was mostly silent. Of course, besides awesomeming327..

"M MMMM MMM MMMM! MMMMMMMM!"

It wasn't long before they arrived at the edge of the ship, though it certainly felt like awesomeming327.'s screaming was going to continue forever.

tikkitamatoa ripped the duct tape off of awesomeming327..

"Any last words?"

"I'M INNOCENT I'M TELLING YOU LET ME G-"

tikkitamatoa put the duct tape back on.

"Okay I regret asking him that question. Let's just toss this trash out."

Osmium_ and summitwei nodded in agreement and threw awesomeming327.'s squirming body overboard. Presumably, to the same place where mark888 went, if any. To a place where the dead are forgotten, even by the dead.

[color=blue]awesomeming327. has been thrown overboard. He was a [color=green]Vanilla Townie[/color].

post_rendered: Night 2 has begun. Please submit your actions, if you have any.
post_canonical: [color=blue]Night 2 has begun. Please submit your actions, if you have any.

post_rendered:
With awesomeming327. thrown overboard, everyone went to sleep slightly more soundly. Everyone, except for a small group of people, all gathered in Osmium_'s room.

"Well, how many people do we have? Five?" Osmium_ asked.

He counted the heads in the room. There were indeed five heads.

"Alright, cool. Let's pool in all our chips and count them."

Everyone tossed in their chips. It came up to a total of 516 chips.

"Alright, that's at least 500, which is what we need to get back to the surface. Well, only one of us can cash in on that."

Everyone nodded in agreement. sudzoof pulled out a revolver.

"Alright, here's the thing we need. I found this lying around in a crate below deck, but it should hopefully still work."

sudzoof loaded a bullet into the gun and spun the chamber. He aimed it at the corner of the room and fired.

*click*

"Wait" etr217 interjected "Are you sure it's a good idea to fire a loaded gun in this room?"

etr217 got his answer soon after.

*click*

*click*

BANG!

A voice could be heard through the wall.

"HEY KEEP IT DOWN IM TRYIN' TO SLEEP 'ERE!"

"Uh…" MY-2 said "Is it too late to back out of this? I mean, it's disrupting the peace."

"NO!" said all the others simultaneously.

"Okay fine. I'll play…"

Osmium_ took the gun from sudzoof.

"Anyways, you all should know how Russian roulette works, right? We'll be playing this until there's only one person alive, so I trust that you all will uphold this, right?"

Everyone else nodded.

"Well, let's load a chamber."

Osmium_ emptied out all the chambers.

"Hey sudzoof do you have another bullet on you?"

sudzoof took a bullet out from his pocket and handed it over to Osmium_. He loaded the bullet into the revolver, then spun the chamber. When the chamber stopped spinning, he put the gun down.

"Alright, I'll spin this, and whoever it points to will shoot first. Then, we will go clockwise. To prevent anyone from chickening out if there's been 5 blanks, after 5 shots, the revolver will be respun. So at any point, you will have at most a 50% chance of dying. Now, I shall spin the gun, and we will begin."

Osmium_ spun the gun. After what felt like an eternity, the gun stopped spinning. It pointed to MY-2.

"So, the order is MY-2, then sudzoof, then Osmium_, then me, then etr217, right?" mathjay asked.

"Yep."

"Alright MY-2, what are you waiting for? Pick up the gun and shoot yourself!"

MY-2 hesitated for a bit. There were four pairs of eyes on him. If MY-2 didn't know what peer pressure felt like, he knew now. He slowly picked up the gun, put his finger on the trigger, and aimed it at his temple. He pulled the trigger.

*click*

MY-2 breathed a sigh of relief, then handed the gun over to sudzoof. The room was eerily silent. After all, by the end of this game, 4 people would be dead.

sudzoof picked up the gun and quickly shot it at himself.

*click*

Osmium_ took the gun. He looked at it closely, then looked around the room for a bit. His head seemed to be wandering elsewhere. After a few seconds, he looked at the revolver, aimed it directly between his eyes, and fired.

BANG!

Osmium_'s body fell to the ground with a thump, with copious amounts of blood flowing out of his head and onto the carpet. Three of the four people alive recoiled a bit.

"You guys not used to death? Even after dying once?" sudzoof said.

"Uh… Yeah. I mean… I haven't seen someone kill themselves before. Why are you so calm?" MY-2 responded.

"Let's just say it's because of my previous life. Well, shall we continue?" sudzoof pulled out another bullet from his pocket and picked up the gun.

"Can we please stop? After seeing Osmium_ die, I don't want to see anyone else dead. I just did this because I wanted to just get back to the overworld, and didn't care for my own self, but I can't bear to see someone else die, especially in such a gruesome way." MY-2 inched slowly away from Osmium_'s body.

"Yea… I'd have to agree with MY-2 here." etr217 said.

"If we're going to end here, do you just want to take back the chips we started with, and just split Osmium_'s chips evenly among us? After all, he lost." mathjay proposed.

etr217 and MY-2 nodded.

"Alright, fine." sudzoof said "If you guys don't want to continue I won't force you to. However…" he picked up the revolver. "I will be taking this with me. For self defense."

mathjay eyed sudzoof cautiously, before he took his share of the chips and walked out of the room. etr217 and MY-2 soon followed, going back to their rooms. Everyone silently agreed to not talk about this unless absolutely necessarily. It was late at night anyways. They didn't want to disrupt the others with another announcement of a death. It's not like there was a murderer on board anyways; after all, awesomeming327. isn't here anymore.

After the rest walked out, sudzoof closed the door, and started feeling up Osmium_'s body. After a while, he found a key. sudzoof carried Osmium_'s body to the bathroom, washed his hands, then locked both the bathroom door and the room door. The body would be discovered, but it felt like it'd be better if it wasn't discovered so quickly. It just felt like another body would bring about too much panic. sudzoof wasn't really scared about being framed for murder; there were 3 other people with him who would corroborate his story. He walked back to his room, locked the door, and slept.

Even though Osmium_ technically killed himself, the four still had some semblance of a guilty conscience, though some more than others. That if they maybe spoke up louder, or just refused to play, Osmium_ would still be alive. The push for selfish greed comes with punishment, and for those whose punishment was not death, it would haunt them for the rest of their time.

Osmium_ has lost Russian roulette. He was a Vanilla Townie.

post_rendered:
This is what I have

1. If you motion-detected scum who submitted kill
Naturally, that scum will try to not be hanged, and claiming VT would almost always result them in being hanged. As a result, that scum would claim JOAT which means the other JOAT will have to counter-claim them. The chance of the other JOAT targeting the scum who submitted the kill is very low, so the best move for scum is to claim JOAT in this case.

2. If you motion-detected scum who did not submit the kill
This is the same case as 1, but the scum might not claim JOAT and plead you to not hang him or her because they are not outed yet and try to fish the other JOAT out too. If they claim JOAT then the other JOAT has to claim, too, to counterclaim them. If they claim VT, then they would likely be the hang still.

3. If you motion-detected VT
The other JOAT would likely not claim because the risk is really low and the person who you target will claim VT, resulting in them being hanged. The VT will obviously not claim JOAT.

4. If you motion-detected JOAT
The person who you motion-detected is the JOAT, so they will always claim JOAT (since there is no downside to doing so at this point. Most likely they will be un-cc'd, so that will result in both JOATs being outed.


More coming later.

[ quote=summitwei]some mechanics stuff:

so i was thinking of revealing my check and asking the other joat to claim *if and only if* they're not my check and didn't check the same person (which means that the check is confirmed scum). i still have my doc left, so i'll save you tonight, and there'll definitely be at least one more check gotten off. if the other joat hit my check or is my check, then everybody will stay silent, and the other joat is not outed in the worst case scenario.[/quote]

I was/am conflicted on what to do in this situation because in the non-messy situation, summit reveals motion on me and, as I am his check, everyone remains silent (and I’m not outed).

Then Kevin claimed so there are only 2 possibilities
1) Kevin is lying
2) summit is lying
3) theres another layer on FPS
I’m willing to completely cross out #3 because it hurts my brain and would be useless and I really can’t see summit being wolf ever? anyways

wolf!kevin knows that in a standard playing out of the situation, JOAT never claims before the targeted player (me) does, which is made pretty obvious in his casework here:
kevinmathz wrote:
This is what I have

1. If you motion-detected scum who submitted kill
Naturally, that scum will try to not be hanged, and claiming VT would almost always result them in being hanged. As a result, that scum would claim JOAT which means the other JOAT will have to counter-claim them. The chance of the other JOAT targeting the scum who submitted the kill is very low, so the best move for scum is to claim JOAT in this case.

2. If you motion-detected scum who did not submit the kill
This is the same case as 1, but the scum might not claim JOAT and plead you to not hang him or her because they are not outed yet and try to fish the other JOAT out too. If they claim JOAT then the other JOAT has to claim, too, to counterclaim them. If they claim VT, then they would likely be the hang still.

3. If you motion-detected VT
The other JOAT would likely not claim because the risk is really low and the person who you target will claim VT, resulting in them being hanged. The VT will obviously not claim JOAT.

4. If you motion-detected JOAT
The person who you motion-detected is the JOAT, so they will always claim JOAT (since there is no downside to doing so at this point. Most likely they will be un-cc'd, so that will result in both JOATs being outed.


More coming later.

Notice that in all of the cases where the check is guilty, he expects the check to claim first and then the JOAT will follow up with a CC

post_rendered:
Everyone was slightly concerned after they heard about how Osmium_ went out, but after a while everyone just got used to it. After all, the dead shouldn't be surprised about death, right?

Near the end of the day, at the twenty-second hour, everyone decided to meet on the deck in order to play some classic Texas hold 'em. Just a fairly low stakes game. Everyone agreed to not bet too high, and to just do this for fun.

And for a while, that agreement was upheld. A few hours in, and the biggest loss was kevinmathz losing 25 chips from a failed bluff, and ██████ gaining those chips. Other than that, everyone's gains and losses were within a score of chips. Then, on the fifteen deal, things started heating up.

snoozingnewt had the dealer button, and the cards were passed out accordingly. Given that this was a low stakes game, the small blind was only 1 chip, and the big blind was only 2. Everyone just called, until it was mathjay's turn. He raised to fifty chips.

"Hey mathjay, what are you doing? I thought we agreed this was a low stakes game?" snoozingnewt asked.

"Well," mathjay responded "I have a good hand. Maybe. Or I'm bluffing. Either way, I just wanted to spice things up. It's getting late, and I want at least one hand to be exciting."

"Well, I'm folding," sudzoof replied.

Everyone else proceeded to do the same. Except for one.

"Well…" tikkitamatoa said "I have a pretty good hand. Some might say, the best. So, I'll call your bluff. I'm not just calling. I'm going ALL IN!"

tikkitamatoa slid his pile of chips towards the center of the table.

"Interesting… I shall do the same." mathjay likewise slid his chips towards the center of the table.

A total of 212 chips were in the pot. Everyone other than tikkitamatoa and mathjay looked on with anticipation. Both players had all in'd. It was down to the heart of the cards.

"Do you want to reveal our hands now?" mathjay proposed. "It's not like it'll affect the outcome."

"Sure, why not. Now, prepare to tremble at my pocket aces!" tikkitamatoa showed his hand. It was an ace of hearts and an ace of diamonds.

"Huh. Not bad. However, you can't count me out just yet!" mathjay revealed a king of spades and a queen of spades.

mathjay clearly had the worse hand. However, it didn't guarantee that tikkitamatoa would win.

The dealer burned a card, then showed the flop. A jack of hearts, a four of spades, and a seven of spades. It was still anyone's game. Another card was burned, and the turn was revealed to be a nine of clubs. One more card was burned. Everyone looked in anticipation. mathjay would win if a spade or a ten was drawn. tikkitamatoa would win in all other situations.

The card drawn. A glimpse of red could be seen, but not enough to determine the card. An eternity later, a few milliseconds later, the card was revealed. Its suit was the heart. Its value was a ten. A ten of hearts.

mathjay took all the chips for himself.

"Well, that was a fun round. I thought I'd almost lost there. tikkitamatoa, my condolences to you. However, unless someone wants to sacrifice the quality of their eternity, I don't think anyone will bail you out. So, just enjoy the time here while it lasts. I'll be going back to my room."

mathjay pocketed all the chips into the briefcase he was holding, then walked out.

tikkitamatoa meanwhile, just stood there. He was bankrupt. There was nothing he could do. No one was willing to help him out. After all, who wouldn't be selfish when not just their lives, but their eternity was on the line?

After a while, tikkitamatoa muttered a phrase. It was quiet, but distinct.

"Notevand grant, knonse do. I ard, shin fore fabour're re!"

Near the end, tikkitamatoa's voice rose to almost a shout.

"Hey tikkitamatoa, are you okay? I think you might be going through some issues after that loss. Come with me, I'll take you to-"

But before snoozingnewt could finish talking, a bright flash appeared on top of tikkitamatoa's body. When the flash subsided, tikkitamatoa was nowhere to be found.

"What the…"

Everyone except mathjay witnessed it. Right there, a body vanished in front of them. Not just a body, but a living person. Well, living in some sense of the word. Presumably, tikkitamatoa went into the afterlife, but no one knows for sure. Everyone has learned to stop questioning the things that happen on the ferry. The only thing that was being questioned was their assumptions, which were broken constantly. After a bit of chattering, everyone agreed to just not worry too much about it. With nothing else to do, and no corpse to dispose of, everyone just silently went back to their rooms, pondering what just happened.

tikkitamatoa has vanished. He was a Mafia Goon.

post_canonical: [color=blue]Night 3 has begun. Please submit your night actions, if you have any.

post_rendered:
With just nine people remaining, everyone got to know each other much better. Everyone would gather around for breakfast, and just discuss random things. Sometimes they would gamble for a bit, but mostly just for fun. Sometimes using virtual chips instead of physical ones.

Today however, they would use real chips. A small computer lab was discovered deep within the ferry, and everyone decided to investigate it for a while.

"Why is there even a computer lab on a ferry? Why didn't we find this thing earlier?" sudzoof remarked.

"Beats me. At least there isn't really the risk of flooding, so that sort of makes sense?" snoozingnewt replied.

summitwei walked up to a computer and tried to turn it on. Nothing.

"There's a power cord over here; let's plug it in." snoozingnewt picked up the plug and inserted it into the wall socket. As soon as he did, all the computers turned on.

Every computer had a generic wallpaper, and a few assorted bits of applications, mostly ones related to gambling. However, there was an "Internet" application that everyone was drawn to.

sudzoof took control of the mouse on one of the computers and clicked it. The home page was some sort of gambling hub. Before sudzoof could read any of the specifics, a pop-up appeared with the following text:

"PLEASE NOTE THAT THE INTERNET ON BOARD THIS SHIP IS DOWNLOAD ONLY. THERE SHALL BE NO TRANSFERRING OF INFORMATION FROM THIS FERRY TO THE OUTSIDE WORLD. THANK YOU FOR YOUR COOPERATION."

Everyone read the text for a bit before sudzoof closed the pop-up. He clicked on a button called "Horse Racing." After a bit of loading, a stream of a horse race popped up.

"Hey this is pretty cool. We can probably try and bet on the horses here; it certainly is different than what we've been doing." snoozingnewt said.

"Wait a second…" kevinmathz peered closer to the screen. "Doesn't that jockey look familiar to any of you?" He pointed to the jockey of horse number five.

"Hmmm. Maybe? I vaguely remember having seen her before, but I can't remember exactly when or where." summitwei said.

Everyone else had similar responses.

"AND THE WINNER IS HORSE NUMBER TWO!" The announcer from the screen announced. "WE'LL BE HOSTING ANOTHER RACE IN JUST TWENTY MINUTES!"

"Well, y'all want to bet on this upcoming race?" sudzoof asked.

Everyone nodded in agreement.

"Alright, let's get our chips ready then. I'll be betting on horse number two!"

All the people present made their bets accordingly. They all seemed to have forgotten what they've lost in life.

Forgotten about the people they've met. People with bodies. Even if those bodies weren't real, their souls were. And indeed, a soul has been forgotten. Forced to be forgotten. Taken before her time was up, and retroactively fixed.

Everyone just happily played as if their time on the ship was endless. However, like all mortal things, good or bad, it must come to an end. The end of the ferry's journey was approaching. Sooner or later, it would catch up to them, and everyone would be judged accordingly.

C̢̫͓͉̺̮̞͈̙͖̰͔̪̰̠͕̹̿̂̐̋ͤ̾̌̈̍ͩͯ̓͡ẖ͍̻̙͚̠͕̈͗ͯ̏ͤ̈͆͗ͧ̚͟͜ą͕̙͉̦̯̱̝̗̱͍̞͐̔ͯ̀ͦ͆̓͐͌ͣ̍ͣ̏̃̀̂́̚͞͝r̵̛͗ͦͨ͏̜̺̠̬̘̳̞̭̫̩͙ͅͅͅg̵̷̰͈͈͔̱͚̠̰̫̯̠̖̺̦̺͂͌͛ͦ͐͐ͤ́̚̕͠ẹ̶̢̧͎̙̦̣̤̦͉̦̼̳̲̘̯̳̘̐ͫ̐̑ͣͯ͒̾͋̏̑͜ has never existed on the ferry. She is a Vanilla Townie.

With 9 people remaining, what better way to spend time than a social deduction game. Specifically, mafia! It was a game that many people have heard of, and with 9 people still existing, what better time to play than now?

Plus, there were no shortage of dealers, as long as the game was being played as a gamble. The rules were simple: the losing faction pays 14 chips to the winning faction, all evenly distributed. Why fourteen? Well two times seven is fourteen, silly!

The setup was a classic setup. Cop9, as some people liked to call it. Some people call it the perfect form of mafia. Others called it a bastardization of their favorite way to play the game. None of those opinions mattered anyways. After all, who'd care that deeply about some childish social deduction game, right?

A few rounds were played with the cards and everything was going normal. However, after a few rounds, a few players stood out. mathjay, MY-2, and snoozingnewt. They were all consistently doing better than the others. After a bit, everyone took a break and discussed the overall metagame. In particular, they wanted to know how these three were doing so well.

"So, mathjay, how are you even doing so well?" sheepsaysmeep asked.

"Well," mathjay responded, "playing mafia is sorta a hobby of mine. At least, I think it was. I have lots of memories of this game. Here, I'll even explain a special mechanic of this setup called cop cover. I didn't want to confuse you all too much, so I decided not to tell you guys until now."

mathjay then went over the basics of cop cover. How even a clueless person's information can be vital to cracking who the delicious wolves are. Everyone was amazed at his knowledge of the game. After he finished, he had a question to ask.

"So you," mathjay directed to MY-2, "why are you doing so well? You said you've never heard of this game before."

"Well…" MY-2 said "let's just say I'm very good at convincing people. I know how to lie, and conversely, I know how to catch them. You all have tells for when you lie and when you tell the truth. Some more than others. But with every game, I catch onto more and more of them. 'What tells?' you ask? Well, I guess I'll keep them a secret for now." MY-2 ended his sentence with a wink.

Everyone turned to snoozingnewt. 8 pairs of eyes were on him. Everyone expected him to have some secret technique to be good at mafia. Well, he did. "I…" snoozingnewt said nervously "will keep it a secret."

An awkward amount of silence fell upon them. They ate some snacks and resumed the game. However, snoozingnewt's secret wouldn't stay hidden for long. As time passed, more and more would be revealed.

"Hey," MY-2 asked snoozingnewt after a round, "you haven't been doing anything fishy, have you?"

"No… Why do you ask? Are you just envious of how good I am?"

"No. Did you know I was a famous poker player in my life?"

"And… How does that relate?"

"Cards." MY-2 said bluntly. "I deal with them. And I know for sure you've marked the cards. Now, don't get me wrong, I've noticed this for a long time. However, I just assumed they were just random scratches. Upon further inspection, I know exactly what you did. You marked the cop card and the two mafia goon cards in a special way. Look at the back of the card you're holding right now."



"Notice something? Of course you do. That's how you figured out how to tell cards apart even when all the cards were marked. How you almost managed to get away with it. I'm saying almost, because while all cards were tampered with in a way that was almost impossible to feel the difference, there is one key difference between the cards you marked as special, and the ones for the vanilla townies. The card backs have no rotational symmetry." MY-2 picked up the card snoozingnewt was holding and put it up right next to his.

"You see? You can turn these cards upside-down. And the special role cards were marked on one side, and the rest were marked on the others. I've caught you red-handed, haven't I, snoozingnewt?"

snoozingnewt was at a loss for words. Everyone else was speechless.

"Hey dealers!" MY-2 shouted "We got a CHEATER here, and I've got proof!"

"Wait wait you don't have to go that far." snoozingnewt said. "I'll just admit it. Yes, I've cheated, and I'm sorry for it. I'll give you back the chips I got, as long as nothing else-"

"HOLD IT RIGHT THERE!" a booming voice said. The same voice at the beginning of the ferry.

"DIDN'T I SAY CHEATING WAS STRICTLY PROHIBITED?" the voice was seemingly near snoozingnewt's ear.

"Stop it! Please! I said I was sorry!" snoozingnewt yelped.

"NO CAN DO! YOU DID THE READ TERMS OF SERVICE THAT WAS PROVIDED IN EACH OF YOUR ROOMS WHEN YOU ARRIVED, RIGHT? AFTER ALL, BY EXISTING HERE, YOU AGREE TO THEM, SO YOU BETTER HAVE READ THEM."

"Please I'll do anything just stop this please! Forgive me!" snoozingnewt pleaded with tears in his eyes.

"NO FORGIVENESS. NOW, IT'S TIME FOR YOUR PUNISHMENT!"

snoozingnewt screamed in agony.

"AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA"

And then the screaming stopped, and he was no more.

snoozingnewt has been punished. He was a Mafia Goon.
